thread.title: Carl Ginapp declared winner of Cerro Gordo Supervisor District 3 seat after recount
thread.title_full: Carl Ginapp declared winner of Cerro Gordo Supervisor District 3 seat after recount

author: ROBIN McCLELLAND Globe Gazette
title: Carl Ginapp declared winner of Cerro Gordo Supervisor District 3 seat after recount
text:
Carl Ginapp has been declared the winner of the Cerro Gordo Supervisor District 3 race.
A recount of votes in the Cerro Gordo County Supervisors District 3 race has found Carl Ginapp to be the winner. An additional vote was counted to put Ginapp in the winner's circle. In total Ginapp received 3,108 votes to Meacham Ginapp's 3,105.
"It's very humbling," Ginapp said of the win following the recount. "It's been such a positive experience from knocking thousands of doors to meeting so many people in Cerro Gordo County. I'm grateful to the voters."
On Election Day, incumbent Democrat Lori Meacham Ginapp led by just two votes once unofficial results were tallied. Once provisional ballots had been counted, that lead switched to Republican challenger Carl Ginapp, again by just two votes.
Today, at the election annex, located at 121 Third St. N.W., representatives of each candidate and a third party observed the recount. Meacham Ginapp selected Timothy Lapointe, and Ginapp selected Don O'Connor. The candidates selected Colleen Frein of the Mason City Chamber of Commerce as the third representative.
People are also reading…
The Cerro Gordo County Supervisors will canvas the results at 2 p.m. on Tuesday, Nov. 26.
